title: NOC 31101 – Specialists in Surgery | GTR Immigration
description: Surgical specialists are among the most sought-after and highest-compensated medical professionals in Canada. With expanding hospital infrastructure, aging demographics, and surgical backlog recovery, demand for qualified surgeons continues across provinces. Classified under NOC 31101 (TEER 1), this occupation aligns with:
duties: Perform invasive and minimally invasive surgical procedures
Provide medical and surgical care related to pregnancy & childbirth
Diagnose and surgically treat eye conditions
Diagnose and treat disorders of the urinary and reproductive systems
Assess patients and determine appropriate surgical procedures
employment_requirements: Graduation from an approved medical school
Certification by the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Canada
Provincial licensure from the appropriate medical regulatory authority
Without provincial licensing, surgeons cannot practice independently in Canada.
job_outlook: Although competitive due to licensing requirements, Canada continues active recruitment—especially for:
🏥 Planning Canada PR as a Surgical Specialist?

GTR Immigration can assess your eligibility under NOC 31101, guide you through Royal College certification and provincial licensing steps, and structure the most strategic immigration pathway into Canada’s healthcare system.
pathways: Access to public healthcare & social benefits
Long-term career stability in a publicly funded health system
additional_information: Acute-care hospitalsSpecialty surgical centersCancer & cardiac institutesTeaching hospitalsRegional health authorities
Typical annual earnings: $100,700–$557,400 CAD, depending on specialization, province, and billing model.
High-demand surgical areas include orthopedics, cardiac surgery, neurosurgery, general surgery, and obstetrics-gynecology.
